name: Ulupo Heiau
tagline: A massive ancient Hawaiian temple platform in Kailua — legend says the Menehune built it in a single night.
parking: Park at the Windward YMCA and walk to the heiau
bestTime: Early morning for quiet reflection — the site faces the Ko'olau mountains
talkStory: This massive stone platform — 140 feet long and 30 feet high — overlooks Kawainui Marsh, once a productive fishpond feeding thousands. Legend says the Menehune built it in a single night, passing basalt stones hand-to-hand from Kailua Bay. It was likely an agricultural heiau dedicated to Lono, god of fertility and harvest, positioned above the marsh to bless the food supply. A short path through a bamboo grove behind the Windward YMCA leads to the base — most Kailua beachgoers have no idea this ancient temple is right behind them.
